# Verza TV

> Verza TV is the first U.S. platform for premium short-form vertical entertainment — serialized, cinematic "micro dramas" told in bite-sized full-screen episodes made for smartphones. Founded in 2024 by Alan Mruvka, creator of E! Entertainment Television. A Filmology Labs company.

## What Verza TV is

Verza TV produces and distributes premium micro dramas: serialized, story-driven episodes shot in full-screen portrait format and released as short installments, typically one to three minutes each. Unlike generic social clips, every series delivers a complete narrative arc with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Episodes are produced in true 1080×1920 resolution for full-screen mobile playback.

The company connects three audiences around an emerging category of entertainment:

- Viewers: addictive, cinematic series across romance, thriller, and drama, available on demand.
- Creators: a premium studio and distribution home for serialized short-form storytelling.
- Brands: native integration and partnership opportunities inside premium micro drama content.

## Key facts

- First U.S. platform dedicated to premium short-form vertical entertainment.
- Founded in 2024 by Alan Mruvka, creator of E! Entertainment Television (a network that reached over 90 million U.S. households).
- Operates as a Filmology Labs company.
- Short-form portrait video accounts for more than 60% of mobile viewing time (industry viewing data as of 2025).
- Content is produced in true 1080×1920 vertical resolution.

## Frequently asked questions

- What is Verza TV? The first U.S. platform dedicated to premium short-form entertainment, delivering serialized micro dramas optimized for mobile viewing.
- Who founded it? Alan Mruvka, the entrepreneur who created E! Entertainment Television, founded the company in 2024.
- How is it different? It is purpose-built for premium micro dramas, delivering complete stories in short full-screen episodes rather than repackaging horizontal or social-media content.
